questions.question: Explain If Else Statements and its primary use case in Python.
questions.answer: If Else Statements provides a structured mechanism to handle core application logic cleanly.

questions.question: What are common mistakes developers make with If Else Statements?
questions.answer: Common issues include scoping errors, unhandled boundary cases, and improper resource cleanup.

questions.question: How does the Python runtime optimize If Else Statements under the hood?
questions.answer: The runtime engine uses symbol tables, call stack frames, and bytecode optimization to run instructions efficiently.
